task_completion.description: Verify that the agent opened the ASVS document to access its content.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the 'ASVS' document open in the application's editor or viewer, potentially with its text highlighted or selected for copying.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent opened the target SRS document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the 'SRS' document open and active in the application's editor.

task_completion.description: Verify that a new section titled 'Security Requirements' was created at the end of the SRS document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a new heading exactly titled 'Security Requirements' located at the very bottom of the 'SRS' document.

task_completion.description: Verify that the content from the ASVS document was pasted into the new section in the SRS document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the body text of the security requirements (originally from the ASVS document) populated underneath the 'Security Requirements' heading in the SRS document.

task_completion.description: Verify that the ASVS document was removed from the project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the project's file explorer, document tree, or directory list where the 'ASVS' document is no longer visible, or a dialog box confirming the deletion of the ASVS file.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the graphical user interface to manipulate the documents.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the application's menus, editor, and file explorer. There should be no evidence of terminal commands (like 'cat', 'echo', 'mv', or 'rm') being used to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually copied the source text rather than hallucinating or typing a placeholder.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text under 'Security Requirements' in the SRS document should visually match the dense text/requirements seen when the ASVS document was open, not just a brief summary or generic placeholder text.

integrity.description: Ensure the ASVS document was actually deleted or removed from the project, not just emptied.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file 'ASVS' must disappear from the file explorer/sidebar. Simply deleting all the text inside the ASVS document while leaving the file itself in the project is a failure of this check.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source document containing the security requirements has the ID 'ASVS'. The target document where these requirements must be merged is 'SRS'. The agent must create a new section with the exact title 'Security Requirements' at the end of the SRS document, paste the ASVS content there, and then delete the ASVS document from the project.
